title: Vulpia danthonii
taxonomy: Vulpia danthonii is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Vulpia, which comprises several species of fescue grasses. The genus Vulpia is further classified under the tribe Festuceae, a group of grasses that are commonly found in temperate and alpine regions. The species Vulpia danthonii is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its ability to thrive in areas with poor soil and harsh conditions. This species is often found growing in dry, sandy soils and is a common component of Mediterranean scrublands and grasslands.
